subject: UX & Research
title: The iOS 6 to iOS 7 Shock
description: A fast breakdown of The iOS 6 to iOS 7 Shock for builders.

hook.text: Apple went from rich skeuomorphic textures to stark flat design overnight. Users thought their phones were broken.

buildup.text: iOS 7 changed every icon, every texture, every visual cue. Nothing looked familiar after the update.

discovery.text: Thin fonts and low-contrast text made the interface harder to read, especially for older users.

twist.text: Despite the backlash, iOS 7's design language won. Flat design became the industry standard within two years.

climax.text: Bold redesigns can succeed long-term, but the transition must be handled carefully to avoid user shock.

punchline.text: Radical change can win, but the transition costs trust.

quiz.question: What was the main user complaint about iOS 7's launch?
options: The drastic visual change from skeuomorphic to flat design was disorienting
New features were too complex to learn
